name: Searle, Michael
resolved_employer: Searle Strategies (Austin public-affairs/political-consulting firm) — Founder & CEO; formerly aide to Austin City Council Member Ellen Troxclair (District 8)
evidence: Filing lists occupation/employer as "SELF"/"CEO" (fields appear swapped, i.e. self-employed CEO) — a distinctive match to Michael Searle, an Austin political consultant who founded and runs Searle Strategies, a public-affairs firm, after serving as an aide to conservative Council Member Ellen Troxclair. He headed "Citizens for an Accountable Austin," the pro-Prop K PAC behind the 2018 city-efficiency-audit ballot measure, funded via roughly $137,000 from the dark-money nonprofit Austin Civic Fund, which he also led as Executive Director/chair; he declined to disclose the fund's donors, prompting a city Ethics Commission review of a possible dark-money-ordinance violation. Austin Chronicle describes him as a leader of ALEC's (American Legislative Exchange Council) local-government affiliate, which seeks partners to privatize municipal services. He also serves on the boards of the Austin Independent Business Alliance and Just Liberty (bipartisan criminal-justice reform) and on the City of Austin Tourism Commission. Identity confidence is medium: the CEO/self-employed occupation pairing is a strong, distinctive match to this public figure, but the Dripping Springs (78620) address was not independently corroborated in the sources found.

affiliations.org: American Legislative Exchange Council (ALEC) — local-government affiliate
affiliations.snippet: Searle is a leader of ALEC's local-government affiliate, which aims to find partners who will "privatize historically municipal services."

affiliations.snippet: Michael Searle is the head of the pro-Prop K political action committee Citizens for an Accountable Austin... a nonprofit called Austin Civic Fund handed over $137,080 to the PAC... Searle has shielded the identities of donors to the Civic Fund.

name: Aleshire, Bill
resolved_employer: AleshireLAW PC — Owner/Attorney (government-accountability & open-records law); former Travis County Tax Assessor-Collector (1980–85) and former Travis County Judge (1987–98)
evidence: Zip 78739 Austin and employer "AleshireLAW PC" match exactly a well-documented local public figure. Bill Aleshire is a former Travis County Tax Collector and former Travis County Judge who opened his solo firm AleshireLAW PC in 2015, focused on government-transparency and open-records litigation; he volunteers as a hotline attorney for the Freedom of Information Foundation of Texas and received that organization's 2025 James Madison Award for decades of open-government advocacy. Recent work includes a successful Texas Open Meetings Act challenge to Hays County's 2024 $439M road-bond election. FEC PAC-contribution, Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist, and topic-specific searches (AIPAC, oil & gas, NRA/guns, defense contractors) returned no matches for this person on any of the tracked policy spectrums.
